summary: Large matrix-scalar kernels can hit vector-core UB out-of-bounds when the scalar is read once from GM and  is used with oversized tiles. Fix by treating the scalar as a broadcast tensor input, switching compute to vector-vector , and increasing TILE_NUM so each tile stays within a conservative UB footprint.

summary: Reducing TILE_NUM from 8 to 256 alone did not eliminate runtime vector-core faults for large matrix-scalar multiplication. The stable fix required both broadcasting the scalar into a tensor path and using much smaller tiles; rely on Mul with an expanded scalar tensor instead of dynamic-scalar Muls for this pattern.

summary: After switching to broadcasted tensor multiply, tile granularity still mattered. On large ND inputs, increasing TILE_NUM to 2048 reduced per-tile workload enough to clear CCU/vector execution faults and pass correctness and performance checks.
